requirements.description: The CLI binary must initialize and be ready to accept user input immediately after execution to ensure a responsive offline experience.
requirements.measurement_method: Measure elapsed time from process start to readiness for input using high-resolution system timers.

requirements.description: All data files created by the application must be restricted to the owning user to prevent unauthorized access or modification by other users.
requirements.measurement_method: Verify file permissions using 'ls -l' command on generated data files immediately after creation.

requirements.description: The application must ensure data integrity is maintained even if the process is terminated unexpectedly during a write operation.
requirements.measurement_method: Simulate write operation followed by immediate process kill (SIGKILL) and verify file state on restart.

requirements.description: The file-based storage system must handle a growing number of tasks without significant performance degradation or file system fragmentation.
requirements.threshold: Support up to 10,000 tasks with ≤ 50ms latency increase
requirements.measurement_method: Load test by incrementing task count and measuring query latency using automated benchmarking scripts.

requirements.description: The application must log all critical events to assist in debugging and monitoring system health.
requirements.threshold: 100% of error events logged with ISO 8601 timestamps
requirements.measurement_method: Review log files during error scenarios to confirm timestamped entries exist and match error codes.

requirements.description: All error messages and help text must be clear and actionable for users with varying technical proficiency.
requirements.threshold: 100% of error messages include a suggested resolution
requirements.measurement_method: Inspect CLI output for all error codes to verify presence of resolution instructions via static analysis.
